original text:
Keto-Mojo Help Docs & FAQs - What does a "Lo" Keto-Mojo meter reading mean?
All Categories ​>​ Frequently Asked Questions, FAQs ​>​ What does a "Lo" Keto-Mojo meter reading mean?
What does a "Lo" Keto-Mojo meter reading mean?
Keto-Mojo meters accurately read ketones down to 0.1 mmol/L. Below that, the meter will show a reading of "Lo," meaning your ketone levels are too low for the meter to detect.
You are not in nutritional ketosis and need to reevaluate your carbohydrate consumption and fat intake. Then, test again in a day.
You have not calibrated your meter with the specific code chip that comes with each pack of ketone strips. Learn how to calibrate your meter here.
Your blood sample size is too small. Learn more here.
What do "Lo" and "Hi" Keto-Mojo meter readings mean?
My Keto-Mojo Meter is showing a PC code. What does it mean?
Why does my Keto-Mojo meter reading keep showing "333"?

Keto-Mojo meters accurately read ketones down to 0.1 mmol/L. Below that, the meter will show a reading of "Lo," meaning your ketone levels are too low for the meter to detect.
You are not in nutritional ketosis and need to reevaluate your carbohydrate consumption and fat intake. Then, test again in a day.

original text:
Squaw Valley Realty -- Providing the Best Real Estate Service and Results for Decades!
Overview: Squaw Valley Realty, originally established in the 1950's, is a small real estate firm that has been in North Lake Tahoe for decades providing quality customer service. They are professional experts with knowledge of the real estate market in Squaw Valley.
Squaw Valley Real Estate can help people sell, rent or buy a home. They have listed and sold hundreds of premier properties and homes throughout the years.
Why We Sell: Squaw Valley Real Estate has decades of experience buying, selling and listing property in the Squaw Valley, Truckee and North Lake Tahoe area.
Professional Guidance: Squaw Valley Realty can help people through the entire process of buying or selling their home, including answering questions about property value, inspections and current mortgage rates.
60+ Years Experience: Squaw Valley Real Estate are the go-to people of real estate because they have decades of professional experience buying and selling homes in Squaw Valley.
Vacation Rentals: Squaw Valley Realty also features hundreds of luxury North Lake Tahoe vacation rental properties.
Inside Knowledge: The 1960 Winter Games were held in Squaw Valley, earning it world recognition. Over the years, Squaw Valley has expanded into more than 3600 acres of skiable slopes.
Lake Tahoe Real Estate has years of experience selling and buying property in North Lake Tahoe, Truckee and Squaw Valley.
Squaw Valley Realty is knowledgeable of the Squaw Valley real estate market and can help people through the home ownership and selling process.
Squaw Valley Realty features hundreds of beautiful Squaw Valley Homes for sale online.
Fun things to do while you're in Squaw Valley.
Need someplace to eat? Check out these Squaw Valley restaurants.
Residents of this home have access to the Tahoe Mountain Club membership which includes free shuttle service to Northstar and access to the member lounge at the Village at Northstar. This two-story homes has wood floors, french doors, stone rock fireplace and spacious outdoor wood deck and barbecue grill.
Golf View. Residents of this home have access to the Tahoe Mountain Club swim and Old Greenwood golf course. This two-story home features high vaulted ceilings and windows, wood interior and marble counter tops in the kitchen.
A luxury estate in North Lake Tahoe, this lakefront property spans more than three acres and has its own private pier and buoy. The two story estate has cathedral ceilings, high ceiling windows that boast lake views, granite and carpet flooring, marble counter and etched glass doors. There are two guest suites with a living room, dining room, full kitchen and fireplaces. The estate also has an office, theater, wine room, separate three car garage, heated stone driveway and more!
visit the official website at http://www.squawvalleyrealty.net/ .
*Some images shown above are not actual photos from the vacation rental, but category representations. Visit the vacation rental company web site for actual photos.

original text:
Comic science fiction or comedy science fiction is a subgenre of soft science fiction or science fantasy that exploits the science-fiction (SF) genre's conventions for comedic effect. Comic science fiction often mocks or satirizes standard SF conventions - such as alien invasion of Earth, interstellar travel, or futuristic technology. It can also satirize and criticize present-day society.
An early example was the Pete Manx series by Henry Kuttner and Arthur K. Barnes (sometimes writing together and sometimes separately, under the house pen-name of Kelvin Kent). Published in Thrilling Wonder Stories in the late 1930s and early 1940s, the series featured a time-traveling carnival barker who uses his con-man abilities to get out of trouble. Two later series cemented Kuttner's reputation as one of the most popular early writers of comic science fiction: the Gallegher series (about a drunken inventor and his narcissistic robot) and the Hogben series (about a family of mutant hillbillies). The former appeared in Astounding Science Fiction in 1943 and 1948 and was collected in hardcover as Robots Have No Tails (Gnome, 1952), and the latter appeared in Thrilling Wonder Stories in the late 1940s. In the 1950s comedy became more common in science fiction.[citation needed] Some of the authors contributing included: Alfred Bester, Harry Harrison, C.M. Kornbluth, Frederik Pohl, and Robert Sheckley.
The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy is a comic science-fiction series written by Douglas Adams. Originally a radio comedy broadcast on BBC Radio 4 in 1978, it later morphed into other formats, including stage shows, novels, comic books, a 1981 TV series, a 1984 computer game, and 2005 feature film. A prominent series in British popular culture, The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy has become an international multi-media phenomenon; the novels are the most widely distributed, having been translated into more than 30 languages by 2005.
Terry Pratchett's 1981 novel Strata also exemplifies the comic science fiction genre.
Red Dwarf primarily consists of a television sitcom that aired on BBC Two between 1988 and 1999, and on Dave since 2009, gaining a cult following. As of 2018[update] eleven full series of the show plus one "special" miniseries have aired. The latest series, dubbed Red Dwarf XII, started airing in October 2017.
^ "Comedy Science Fiction". Sfbook.com. Retrieved 2016-03-02.
^ "Jo Kent saves cult hg2g game from scrapheap". Retrieved 2014-06-24.
^ "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy". Douglasadams.com. Retrieved 2014-06-24.
^ Gaiman, Neil (2003). Don't Panic: Douglas Adams and the "Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy". Titan Books. pp. 144-145. ISBN 1-84023-742-2.
^ "The Ultimate Reference Guide to British Popular Culture". Oxford Royale. 23 November 2016.
^ Moody, Nickianne (2016). Matthews, Nicole, ed. Judging a Book by Its Cover: Fans, Publishers, Designers, and the Marketing of Fiction. Routledge. ISBN 9781351924672. Retrieved 2018-04-28. Pratchett was associated with irreverent and comic writing which is an established sub genre in science fiction - for example Strata (1982) a parody of Larry Niven's Ringworld[,] a classic science fiction series.
^ "Worldwide Press Office - Red Dwarf on DVD". BBC. Retrieved 28 November 2009.
^ "Red Dwarf XII". Retrieved 21 August 2017.
